seo.title: Building Headless CMS comparison and selection with Conte...
seo.description: A step-by-step implementation guide for Headless CMS comparison and selection with Contentful and Sanity.

content.intro: This guide provides a structured approach to implementing a headless CMS solution for content-heavy sites, focusing on practical decisions for Contentful, Sanity, Strapi, or Payload. Emphasizes content modeling, AI integration, and performance considerations.
prerequisites: Understanding of static site generator workflows

steps.title: Define content modeling requirements
steps.explanation: Create a content type matrix mapping required fields, relationships, and validation rules. Use JSON schema for complex structures. Example: Articles require title (text), author (reference), and content (rich text).

steps.title: Set up CMS environment
steps.explanation: Provision CMS instance with API keys. Configure content delivery API endpoints. Use Sanity's CLI or Contentful's API explorer for initial setup.

steps.title: Integrate with static site generator
steps.explanation: Configure SSG (Next.js/Astro) to pull content via CMS API. Use GraphQL for Sanity or REST for Contentful. Implement incremental static regeneration for updates.
pitfalls: Forgetting to set up proper API access controls
Overloading single query with too many nested relationships

steps.title: Implement AI content generation
steps.explanation: Add AI-powered content suggestions via CMS. Use OpenAI's API for draft generation. Store generated content in CMS with review workflow.

code.content:
import openai
response = openai.Completion.create(engine="davinci", prompt="Write an article about CMS")

pitfalls: Not validating AI-generated content before publishing
Ignoring token cost limits in production

steps.title: Optimize performance for scale
steps.explanation: Implement caching layers (Vercel/Netlify edge functions). Use CDN for static assets. Monitor query performance with CMS analytics tools.
pitfalls: Not setting up proper cache headers
Ignoring database indexing for frequently queried fields

steps.title: Plan CMS migration strategy
steps.explanation: Export content via CMS API or CLI. Map old structure to new content model. Use JSON or CSV for data transfer. Test SEO slugs and redirects.

content.conclusion: Follow these steps to implement a scalable CMS solution. Focus on content modeling consistency, API performance, and migration testing. Regularly audit content structures as requirements evolve.
